name: TaskFlow
goals: Ship MVP with auth, task CRUD, and team invites
Deploy to Vercel with Supabase backend
Get 10 beta users by end of March

progress.action: Set up Next.js project with Supabase auth
progress.result: Login and signup working, Google OAuth configured

progress.action: Built task CRUD API routes and database schema
progress.result: Create, read, update, delete all working. RLS policies set.

progress.action: Built task list UI with drag-and-drop reordering
progress.result: Kanban board working but slow on 100+ tasks

progress.action: Fixed performance - virtualized task list, added pagination
progress.result: Smooth scrolling even with 500 tasks. Load time under 200ms.

progress.action: Built team invite flow - email invites with magic links
progress.result: Invites send and accept correctly. Role-based access working.
progress.next: Add real-time task updates via Supabase subscriptions

progress.action: Added real-time subscriptions for task updates
progress.result: Changes from one user show instantly for all team members
progress.next: Write tests for API routes before deploying

progress.action: Wrote API route tests - 14 tests covering all CRUD + auth + RLS
progress.result: All passing. Found and fixed a bug where deleted tasks were still visible to non-owners.

progress.action: Deployed to Vercel staging, configured Supabase production project
progress.result: Staging live. Auth works. One issue: magic links redirect to localhost in staging.
progress.next: Fix magic link redirect URL, then deploy to production

failures.what: Drag-and-drop caused full re-render of 200+ task cards
failures.why: Each card was reading from the global state on every position change
failures.learned: Memoize card components and use local state for drag position, sync to global only on drop

failures.what: RLS policy allowed team members to delete tasks they did not own
failures.why: Policy checked team membership but not task ownership
failures.learned: Always test RLS with multiple user roles - owner, member, and outsider

failures.what: Magic links in emails redirect to localhost:3000 instead of staging URL
failures.why: Supabase site URL was still set to localhost in the dashboard
failures.learned: Add Supabase site URL to deployment checklist - it does not auto-detect from Vercel

blockers: Magic link redirect URL needs fixing in Supabase dashboard before production deploy
